 {"id":"bd-705","title":"Implement sanitize_path_segment()","description":"**Sanitize path components**

## What
Remove or replace unsafe characters from path segments.

## Why
Malicious or unusual repo names could cause filesystem issues. Defense in depth.

## Implementation
```bash
sanitize_path_segment() {
    local segment="$1"
    # Remove leading/trailing whitespace
    segment="${segment#"${segment%%[![:space:]]*}"}"
    segment="${segment%"${segment##*[![:space:]]}"}"
    # Replace potentially problematic characters
    segment="${segment//\//_}"
    segment="${segment//\\/_}"
    segment="${segment//:/_}"
    echo "$segment"
}
```

## Characters to Handle
- Slashes (/ \)
- Colons (problematic on Windows)
- Leading dots (hidden files)
- Control characters

## Acceptance Criteria
- Dangerous characters replaced
- Empty segments rejected
- Normal names pass through unchanged","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:56:12.946839267-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:56:12.946839267-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented sanitize_path_segment() with comprehensive character replacement and safety checks","labels":["parsing","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-705","depends_on_id":"bd-7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.707274655-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

+{"id":"bd-73ys","title":"Implement discovery summary display and formatting","description":"# Task: Implement Discovery Summary Display

## Purpose
Format and display discovered work items in a user-friendly summary, especially for --dry-run mode. This gives users visibility into what will be reviewed before sessions start.

## Background
After GraphQL discovery and priority scoring, users need to see:
- Total work items found across all repos
- Breakdown by priority level (CRITICAL/HIGH/NORMAL/LOW)
- Breakdown by type (issues vs PRs)
- Top N items that will be processed
- Repos with most activity

## Implementation

### show_discovery_summary()
```bash
show_discovery_summary() {
    local -n items_ref=$1
    local max_repos="${2:-$REVIEW_PARALLEL}"
    
    # Count totals
    local total=${#items_ref[@]}
    local critical=0 high=0 normal=0 low=0
    local issues=0 prs=0
    
    for item in "${items_ref[@]}"; do
        IFS="|" read -r repo type number title score level _ _ <<< "$item"
        case "$level" in
            CRITICAL) ((critical++)) ;;
            HIGH) ((high++)) ;;
            NORMAL) ((normal++)) ;;
            LOW) ((low++)) ;;
        esac
        case "$type" in
            issue) ((issues++)) ;;
            pr) ((prs++)) ;;
        esac
    done
    
    # Display summary
    if [[ "$GUM_AVAILABLE" == "true" ]]; then
        show_discovery_summary_gum
    else
        show_discovery_summary_ansi
    fi
}
```

### show_discovery_summary_gum()
```bash
show_discovery_summary_gum() {
    gum style --border rounded --padding "1 2" --border-foreground "#fab387" \
        "$(gum style --bold "Discovery Summary")"
    
    echo ""
    gum style "Total work items: $total"
    gum style "  Issues: $issues | PRs: $prs"
    echo ""
    
    # Priority breakdown with colors
    [[ $critical -gt 0 ]] && gum style --foreground "#f38ba8" "  CRITICAL: $critical"
    [[ $high -gt 0 ]] && gum style --foreground "#fab387" "  HIGH: $high"
    [[ $normal -gt 0 ]] && gum style --foreground "#f9e2af" "  NORMAL: $normal"
    [[ $low -gt 0 ]] && gum style --foreground "#6c7086" "  LOW: $low"
    echo ""
    
    # Top items preview
    gum style --bold "Top $max_repos items to review:"
    local i=0
    for item in "${items_ref[@]:0:$max_repos}"; do
        ((i++))
        IFS="|" read -r repo type number title score level _ _ <<< "$item"
        local badge
        badge=$(format_priority_badge "$level")
        echo "  $i. $badge $repo#$number: ${title:0:50}"
    done
}
```

### show_discovery_summary_ansi()
ANSI fallback version using escape codes:
```bash
show_discovery_summary_ansi() {
    local BOLD="\033[1m"
    local RED="\033[31m"
    local ORANGE="\033[33m"
    local YELLOW="\033[93m"
    local GRAY="\033[90m"
    local RESET="\033[0m"
    
    echo -e "${BOLD}Discovery Summary${RESET}"
    echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
    echo "Total work items: $total"
    echo "  Issues: $issues | PRs: $prs"
    echo ""
    echo "By priority:"
    [[ $critical -gt 0 ]] && echo -e "  ${RED}CRITICAL: $critical${RESET}"
    [[ $high -gt 0 ]] && echo -e "  ${ORANGE}HIGH: $high${RESET}"
    [[ $normal -gt 0 ]] && echo -e "  ${YELLOW}NORMAL: $normal${RESET}"
    [[ $low -gt 0 ]] && echo -e "  ${GRAY}LOW: $low${RESET}"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${BOLD}Top items to review:${RESET}"
    # ... similar item listing
}
```

### format_priority_badge()
```bash
format_priority_badge() {
    local level="$1"
    if [[ "$GUM_AVAILABLE" == "true" ]]; then
        case "$level" in
            CRITICAL) gum style --foreground "#f38ba8" --bold "CRITICAL" ;;
            HIGH) gum style --foreground "#fab387" "HIGH" ;;
            NORMAL) gum style --foreground "#f9e2af" "NORMAL" ;;
            LOW) gum style --foreground "#6c7086" "LOW" ;;
        esac
    else
        echo "[$level]"
    fi
}
```

### JSON Output Mode
```bash
show_discovery_summary_json() {
    jq -n \
        --argjson total "$total" \
        --argjson issues "$issues" \
        --argjson prs "$prs" \
        --argjson critical "$critical" \
        --argjson high "$high" \
        --argjson normal "$normal" \
        --argjson low "$low" \
        --argjson items "$(printf "%s\n" "${items_ref[@]}" | head -20 | jq -R -s "split(\"\n\") | map(select(. \!= \"\"))")" \
        "{
            total: \$total,
            by_type: {issues: \$issues, prs: \$prs},
            by_priority: {critical: \$critical, high: \$high, normal: \$normal, low: \$low},
            top_items: \$items
        }"
}
```

## Testing
- Verify gum formatting renders correctly
- Verify ANSI fallback works without gum
- Verify JSON output is valid
- Verify priority colors are correct
- Test with 0 items, 1 item, many items

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Summary displays item counts correctly
- [ ] Priority breakdown shows correct colors
- [ ] Top items listed with truncated titles
- [ ] Works with both gum and ANSI fallback
- [ ] JSON output mode for automation

 {"id":"bd-904","title":"Implement detect_collisions()","description":"**Warn about path collisions**

## What
Function to detect when different repos would resolve to the same local path.

## Why
With flat layout, org1/utils and org2/utils collide. Users need to know before we overwrite.

## Difference from dedupe
- dedupe: Same repo referenced twice (ok, skip second)
- collision: Different repos mapping to same path (warning!)

## Implementation
```bash
detect_collisions() {
    local -A path_to_url
    local collisions=0
    
    while IFS= read -r spec; do
        local url branch local_name
        parse_repo_spec "$spec" url branch local_name
        
        local path
        path=$(url_to_local_path "$url" "$PROJECTS_DIR" "$LAYOUT")
        
        local norm_url
        norm_url=$(normalize_url "$url")
        
        if [[ -n "${path_to_url[$path]:-}" ]]; then
            local existing="${path_to_url[$path]}"
            if [[ "$existing" != "$norm_url" ]]; then
                log_warn "Collision detected: $path"
                log_warn "  URL 1: $existing"
                log_warn "  URL 2: $norm_url"
                ((collisions++))
            fi
        else
            path_to_url[$path]="$norm_url"
        fi
    done
    
    return $collisions
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Detects different repos at same path
- Doesn't flag same repo twice
- Returns collision count","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:33:03.266022614-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:33:03.266022614-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all repo list management functions: parse_repo_spec (namerefs), load_repo_list, dedupe_repos, detect_collisions, get_all_repos.  {"id":"bd-905","title":"Implement get_all_repos()","description":"**Load and merge all repository lists**

## What
Function to load all list files, merge, dedupe, and check for collisions.

## Why
This is the main entry point for getting the list of repos to process.

## Implementation
```bash
get_all_repos() {
    local repos_dir="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/repos.d"
    
    if [[ ! -d "$repos_dir" ]]; then
        log_warn "No repos.d directory. Run 'ru init' first."
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Collect all repos from all .txt files
    local all_repos
    all_repos=$(
        for f in "$repos_dir"/*.txt; do
            [[ -f "$f" ]] || continue
            load_repo_list "$f"
        done
    )
    
    if [[ -z "$all_repos" ]]; then
        log_warn "No repositories configured. Run 'ru add <repo>' first."
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Check for collisions (warning only)
    echo "$all_repos" | detect_collisions || true
    
    # Dedupe and output
    echo "$all_repos" | dedupe_repos
}
```

 {"id":"bd-905b","title":"Define collision handling policy","description":"**Clear policy for path collision handling**

## What
Define what happens when collision is detected (different repos -> same path).

## Why
Current plan detects collisions but doesn't specify the behavior.

## Policy Options
1. **Warn and skip second repo** (safest, current implicit behavior)
2. **Fail fast** (strict, stops processing)
3. **Prompt user** (interactive only)
4. **Auto-rename with suffix** (risky)

## Chosen Policy: Warn and skip
```
Collision detected:
  Path: /home/user/projects/utils
  Configured: org1/utils (will be synced)
  Skipped:    org2/utils (same path)

To fix: Change layout to 'owner-repo' in config:
  ru config --set LAYOUT=owner-repo
```

## Implementation
- First occurrence wins
- Log warning with resolution hint
- Include in summary as 'skipped:collision'
- Exit code 2 (conflicts) if any collisions

## Acceptance Criteria
- Clear messaging about collision
- First-wins policy
- Resolution hint in output

 {"id":"bd-9s7y","title":"Implement local driver (tmux + stream-json)","description":"# Task: Implement Local Driver (tmux + stream-json)

## Purpose
Implement the session driver interface using tmux and Claude's stream-json output mode. This is the fallback driver when ntm is not available.

## Background
Claude Code supports --output-format stream-json which emits NDJSON events:
- System init with session_id and available tools
- Assistant messages with text and tool_use
- Tool results
- Final result with status

We capture this output and parse it for question detection.

## Implementation

### local_driver_start_session()
```bash
local_driver_start_session() {
    local wt_path="$1"
    local session_name="$2"
    local prompt="$3"
    
    local log_file="$wt_path/.ru/session.log"
    local event_pipe="$wt_path/.ru/events.pipe"
    
    # Create named pipe for event streaming
    rm -f "$event_pipe"
    mkfifo "$event_pipe"
    
    # Build claude command
    local claude_cmd="claude -p $(printf '%q' "$prompt") --output-format stream-json"
    
    # Create tmux session
    tmux new-session -d -s "$session_name" -c "$wt_path" \
        "exec $claude_cmd 2>&1 | tee '$log_file' > '$event_pipe'"
    
    # Return session info
    cat << EOF
{
  "session_id": "$session_name",
  "worktree": "$wt_path",
  "log_file": "$log_file",
  "event_pipe": "$event_pipe",
  "started_at": "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
}
EOF
}
```

### local_driver_send_to_session()
```bash
local_driver_send_to_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local message="$2"
    
    # Send via tmux
    tmux send-keys -t "$session_id" "$message" Enter
    
    return $?
}
```

### local_driver_get_session_state()
```bash
local_driver_get_session_state() {
    local session_id="$1"
    
    # Check if tmux session exists
    if ! tmux has-session -t "$session_id" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo '{"state": "complete", "reason": "session_ended"}'
        return
    fi
    
    # Capture last lines of output
    local output
    output=$(tmux capture-pane -t "$session_id" -p -S -50 2>/dev/null)
    
    # Detect state from output
    local state="generating"
    local wait_reason=""
    
    # Check for prompt (waiting)
    if echo "$output" | grep -qE '^\s*>\s*$|Press Enter|Select.*:|Enter.*:'; then
        state="waiting"
        wait_reason="prompt_detected"
    fi
    
    # Check for errors
    if echo "$output" | grep -qiE 'error:|panic:|rate.limit|429'; then
        state="error"
    fi
    
    cat << EOF
{
  "state": "$state",
  "wait_reason": "$wait_reason",
  "session_id": "$session_id"
}
EOF
}
```

### local_driver_stream_events()
```bash
local_driver_stream_events() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local callback="$2"
    local wt_path="$3"
    
    local event_pipe="$wt_path/.ru/events.pipe"
    local log_file="$wt_path/.ru/session.log"
    
    # Parse stream-json events
    while IFS= read -r line; do
        [[ -z "$line" ]] && continue
        
        # Parse JSON event type
        local event_type
        event_type=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.type // empty' 2>/dev/null)
        
        case "$event_type" in
            system)
                local session_id_from_event
                session_id_from_event=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.session_id // empty')
                $callback "init" "$session_id_from_event"
                ;;
            assistant)
                # Check for AskUserQuestion tool_use
                local tool_name
                tool_name=$(echo "$line" | jq -r \
                    '.message.content[]? | select(.type=="tool_use") | .name // empty' 2>/dev/null)
                
                if [[ "$tool_name" == "AskUserQuestion" ]]; then
                    local question_data
                    question_data=$(echo "$line" | jq -c \
                        '.message.content[] | select(.name=="AskUserQuestion") | .input')
                    $callback "question" "$question_data"
                fi
                ;;
            result)
                local status
                status=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.status // "unknown"')
                $callback "complete" "$status"
                break
                ;;
        esac
    done < "$event_pipe"
}
```

### local_driver_stop_session()
```bash
local_driver_stop_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
    
    # Kill tmux session
    tmux kill-session -t "$session_id" 2>/dev/null || true
    
    return 0
}
```

### local_driver_interrupt_session()
```bash
local_driver_interrupt_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
    
    # Send Ctrl+C
    tmux send-keys -t "$session_id" C-c
    
    return 0
}
```

## Session Monitoring Loop
```bash
local_driver_monitor_sessions() {
    local -a sessions=("$@")
    
    while [[ ${#sessions[@]} -gt 0 ]]; do
        local -a active_sessions=()
        
        for session_id in "${sessions[@]}"; do
            local state
            state=$(local_driver_get_session_state "$session_id" | jq -r '.state')
            
            case "$state" in
                complete)
                    handle_session_complete "$session_id"
                    ;;
                error)
                    handle_session_error "$session_id"
                    ;;
                waiting)
                    handle_session_waiting "$session_id"
                    active_sessions+=("$session_id")
                    ;;
                *)
                    active_sessions+=("$session_id")
                    ;;
            esac
        done
        
        sessions=("${active_sessions[@]}")
        sleep 2
    done
}
```

## Testing
- Launch session, verify tmux created
- Send message, verify delivered
- Detect AskUserQuestion event
- Detect session completion
- Interrupt and stop work correctly

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sessions launch in tmux with stream-json
- [ ] Events parsed from NDJSON stream
- [ ] AskUserQuestion detected
- [ ] Messages sent via tmux send-keys
- [ ] Session cleanup works

 {"id":"bd-9xjc","title":"Phase 6: Error Handling and Recovery","description":"Phase 6: Error Handling and Recovery

Overview
--------
Robust error handling, retry strategies, checkpointing, and recovery
mechanisms to handle the many failure modes in a multi-session system.

Why Critical?
-------------
Many things can fail:
- Network timeouts
- API rate limits (GitHub, Anthropic)
- Session crashes
- User interrupts (Ctrl+C)
- Disk full
- Authentication expiry

Without proper handling, partial work is lost.

Components
----------

6.1 Error Categories
   Classify errors for appropriate handling:
   - Transient: retry with backoff
   - Rate Limit: backoff + rotate
   - Auth: alert user, pause
   - Session: restart, preserve state
   - User: graceful handling
   - Fatal: abort with clear message

6.2 Retry with Exponential Backoff
   For transient failures:
   - Base delay with exponential growth
   - Jitter to prevent thundering herd
   - Max attempts limit
   - Configurable per operation

6.3 Checkpointing
   Periodic state saves for recovery:
   - After each significant operation
   - Includes all necessary context
   - Atomic writes prevent corruption
   - Configurable interval

6.4 Resume from Checkpoint
   Continue interrupted reviews:
   - Load checkpoint state
   - Identify completed/pending work
   - Skip already-done repos
   - Restore question queue

6.5 Graceful Degradation
   When advanced features unavailable:
   - ntm missing: use local driver
   - gum missing: use ANSI fallback
   - Tests fail: continue without push
   - Rate limited: reduce parallelism

6.6 Signal Handling
   Proper cleanup on interrupts:
   - SIGINT (Ctrl+C): save state, exit
   - SIGTERM: save state, exit
   - SIGHUP: continue in background?
   - Set SYNC_INTERRUPTED flag

Exit Criteria
-------------
- All error categories handled
- Retry logic works correctly
- Checkpoints saved periodically
- Resume continues from checkpoint
- Graceful degradation works
- Signals handled cleanly

+{"id":"bd-cutq","title":"Implement per-repo review policy configuration","description":"# Task: Implement Per-Repo Review Policy Configuration

## Purpose
Allow users to customize review behavior per repository through configuration files. Different repos have different test commands, lint requirements, and review policies.

## Background
Not all repos are the same:
- Some have Makefile, others use npm
- Some require strict lint, others are more lenient
- Some repos should never auto-push
- Some repos need specific branch protection rules

## Configuration Structure

### Directory Layout
```
~/.config/ru/review-policies.d/
├── _default.conf          # Default policy (applies to all)
├── owner_repo1.conf       # Policy for owner/repo1
├── owner_repo2.conf       # Policy for owner/repo2
└── team_*.conf            # Glob patterns work too
```

### Policy File Format
```bash
# ~/.config/ru/review-policies.d/myorg_backend.conf

# Test configuration
REVIEW_TEST_CMD="make test"
REVIEW_TEST_TIMEOUT=300          # seconds

# Lint configuration
REVIEW_LINT_CMD="npm run lint"
REVIEW_LINT_REQUIRED=true        # Block push if lint fails

# Secret scanning
REVIEW_SECRET_SCAN=true
REVIEW_SECRET_PATTERNS="AWS_|PRIVATE_KEY|password"

# Push policy
REVIEW_ALLOW_PUSH=true           # false = never push for this repo
REVIEW_REQUIRE_APPROVAL=false    # true = always ask before push

# Priority overrides
REVIEW_BASE_PRIORITY=50          # Add to all items from this repo
REVIEW_LABELS_BOOST="urgent:30,security:40"

# Review behavior
REVIEW_MAX_ITEMS=10              # Max items to review per session
REVIEW_SKIP_PRS=false            # true = only review issues
REVIEW_DEEP_MODE=false           # true = comprehensive review
```

### Default Policy
```bash
# ~/.config/ru/review-policies.d/_default.conf

REVIEW_TEST_CMD=""               # Auto-detect
REVIEW_LINT_CMD=""
REVIEW_LINT_REQUIRED=false
REVIEW_SECRET_SCAN=true
REVIEW_ALLOW_PUSH=true
REVIEW_REQUIRE_APPROVAL=false
REVIEW_BASE_PRIORITY=0
REVIEW_MAX_ITEMS=20
REVIEW_SKIP_PRS=false
REVIEW_DEEP_MODE=false
```

## Implementation

### load_repo_policy()
```bash
load_repo_policy() {
    local repo_id="$1"
    local policy_dir="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/review-policies.d"
    
    # Reset to defaults first
    source_default_policy
    
    # Try exact match
    local exact_file="$policy_dir/${repo_id//\//_}.conf"
    if [[ -f "$exact_file" ]]; then
        source "$exact_file"
        log_verbose "Loaded policy: $exact_file"
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Try glob patterns (owner_*.conf, team_*.conf)
    local owner="${repo_id%%/*}"
    for pattern_file in "$policy_dir"/"${owner}"_*.conf "$policy_dir"/*_*.conf; do
        if [[ -f "$pattern_file" ]]; then
            # Check if pattern matches
            local pattern_base
            pattern_base=$(basename "$pattern_file" .conf)
            if [[ "$repo_id" == ${pattern_base//_/\/} || \
                  "$repo_id" == ${pattern_base//_/}* ]]; then
                source "$pattern_file"
                log_verbose "Loaded policy via pattern: $pattern_file"
                return 0
            fi
        fi
    done
    
    log_verbose "Using default policy for $repo_id"
    return 0
}
```

### source_default_policy()
```bash
source_default_policy() {
    local default_file="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/review-policies.d/_default.conf"
    
    # Built-in defaults
    REVIEW_TEST_CMD=""
    REVIEW_TEST_TIMEOUT=300
    REVIEW_LINT_CMD=""
    REVIEW_LINT_REQUIRED=false
    REVIEW_SECRET_SCAN=true
    REVIEW_ALLOW_PUSH=true
    REVIEW_REQUIRE_APPROVAL=false
    REVIEW_BASE_PRIORITY=0
    REVIEW_LABELS_BOOST=""
    REVIEW_MAX_ITEMS=20
    REVIEW_SKIP_PRS=false
    REVIEW_DEEP_MODE=false
    
    # Override with user defaults if present
    [[ -f "$default_file" ]] && source "$default_file"
}
```

### apply_policy_priority_boost()
```bash
apply_policy_priority_boost() {
    local repo_id="$1"
    local base_score="$2"
    local labels="$3"
    
    load_repo_policy "$repo_id"
    
    local score=$base_score
    
    # Add base priority boost
    score=$((score + REVIEW_BASE_PRIORITY))
    
    # Apply label boosts
    if [[ -n "$REVIEW_LABELS_BOOST" ]]; then
        IFS="," read -ra boosts <<< "$REVIEW_LABELS_BOOST"
        for boost in "${boosts[@]}"; do
            local label="${boost%%:*}"
            local points="${boost##*:}"
            if echo "$labels" | grep -qi "$label"; then
                score=$((score + points))
            fi
        done
    fi
    
    echo "$score"
}
```

### validate_policy_file()
```bash
validate_policy_file() {
    local file="$1"
    
    # Check syntax
    if ! bash -n "$file" 2>/dev/null; then
        log_error "Syntax error in policy file: $file"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check for required variables
    source "$file"
    
    # Validate values
    if [[ -n "$REVIEW_TEST_TIMEOUT" ]] && ! [[ "$REVIEW_TEST_TIMEOUT" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
        log_error "Invalid REVIEW_TEST_TIMEOUT in $file"
        return 1
    fi
    
    return 0
}
```

### init_review_policies()
```bash
init_review_policies() {
    local policy_dir="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/review-policies.d"
    
    if [[ ! -d "$policy_dir" ]]; then
        mkdir -p "$policy_dir"
        
        # Create example default policy
        cat > "$policy_dir/_default.conf.example" << "EOF"
# Default review policy (rename to _default.conf to activate)
# These settings apply to all repos unless overridden

REVIEW_TEST_CMD=""               # Auto-detect (Makefile, package.json, etc.)
REVIEW_LINT_CMD=""               # Optional lint command
REVIEW_SECRET_SCAN=true          # Scan for secrets before push
REVIEW_ALLOW_PUSH=true           # Allow pushing changes
REVIEW_REQUIRE_APPROVAL=false    # Require confirmation before push
EOF
        log_info "Created example policy file: $policy_dir/_default.conf.example"
    fi
}
```

## Integration Points
- Called by priority scoring (apply_policy_priority_boost)
- Called by quality gates (load test/lint commands)
- Called by push phase (check REVIEW_ALLOW_PUSH)
- Called during cmd_review init

## Testing
- Verify default policy applied
- Verify exact match policy overrides default
- Verify glob pattern matching works
- Verify invalid policy files rejected
- Verify priority boosts applied correctly

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Default policy applied to all repos
- [ ] Per-repo policies override defaults
- [ ] Glob patterns work for org-wide settings
- [ ] Policy validation catches errors
- [ ] Example policy created on init

 {"id":"bd-gptu","title":"Implement rate-limit governor (adaptive concurrency)","description":"Task: Implement Rate-Limit Governor

Purpose
-------
Dynamically adjust parallelism based on real rate limit data from GitHub
API and model responses, preventing throttling and cascading failures.

Background: Why Adaptive?
-------------------------
Static parallelism is dangerous:
- Too high: hit rate limits, get blocked
- Too low: waste time when limits are fine
- Limits vary by time, account tier, prior usage

Solution: query real limits, adjust dynamically.

Governor State
--------------
GOVERNOR_STATE associative array:
- github_remaining: remaining API calls
- github_reset: when limit resets (epoch)
- model_in_backoff: currently backing off?
- model_backoff_until: when backoff ends
- effective_parallelism: current allowed sessions
- circuit_breaker_open: emergency stop?

Implementation
--------------

start_rate_limit_governor()
  Run in background during review:
  while review lock held:
    update_github_rate_limit
    check_model_rate_limit
    adjust_parallelism
    sleep 30

update_github_rate_limit()
  Query: gh api rate_limit
  Extract: .resources.core.remaining and .reset
  If remaining < 500: log warning, reduce parallelism

check_model_rate_limit()
  Scan session logs for 429/rate limit patterns
  If multiple recent hits:
    Set model_in_backoff = true
    Set backoff_until = now + 60 seconds

adjust_parallelism()
  Start with target = REVIEW_PARALLEL (default 4)
  
  If github_remaining < 1000: target /= 2
  If model_in_backoff: target = 1
  
  Count recent errors in sliding window
  If errors > 5 in 5 minutes:
    Open circuit breaker
    target = 0
    Log emergency pause
  
  Set effective_parallelism = target

can_start_new_session()
  Return false if:
  - circuit_breaker_open
  - model_in_backoff
  - active_sessions >= effective_parallelism
  Otherwise true

Circuit Breaker
---------------
Pattern for handling cascading failures:
- CLOSED: normal operation
- OPEN: too many failures, pause all
- HALF-OPEN: after cooldown, try one (not implemented yet)

Metrics Tracked
---------------
- API calls per minute
- 429 responses per session
- Session error rate
- Effective vs requested parallelism

Integration Points
------------------
- Called before starting new session
- Called in session monitor loop
- Updates TUI status display
- Writes metrics for analytics

Testing
-------
- Mock gh api rate_limit responses
- Simulate 429 responses
- Verify parallelism adjusts
- Verify circuit breaker triggers

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Queries real GitHub rate limits
- [ ] Detects model rate limits from logs
- [ ] Adjusts parallelism dynamically
- [ ] Circuit breaker prevents cascading failures
- [ ] Metrics tracked for analysis","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:38:47.005795803-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:38:47.005795803-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-gptu","depends_on_id":"bd-k1kx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:11.712618299-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-gptu","depends_on_id":"bd-9s7y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:11.733398485-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-jm89","title":"Define unified session driver interface","description":"# Task: Define Unified Session Driver Interface

## Purpose
Create an abstract interface that both ntm and local drivers implement, enabling graceful degradation and consistent behavior regardless of which driver is used.

## Background: Why a Unified Interface?
- ntm provides advanced orchestration but may not be installed
- Local driver (tmux + stream-json) works everywhere
- Same code should work with either driver
- Enables testing without full ntm dependency
- Future drivers (remote, cloud) can plug in

## Interface Definition

### Driver Capabilities
```bash
# Query what driver can do
driver_capabilities() {
    # Returns JSON with capabilities
    cat << EOF
{
  "name": "local",
  "parallel_sessions": true,
  "activity_detection": false,
  "health_monitoring": false,
  "question_routing": true
}
EOF
}
```

### Core Operations
```bash
# Start a new Claude session
# Args: worktree_path, session_name, initial_prompt
# Returns: session_id
driver_start_session() {
    local wt_path="$1"
    local session_name="$2"
    local prompt="$3"
    # Implementation varies by driver
}

# Send message/answer to session
# Args: session_id, message
# Returns: 0 on success
driver_send_to_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local message="$2"
}

# Get current session state
# Args: session_id
# Returns: JSON with state info
driver_get_session_state() {
    local session_id="$1"
    # Returns: {"state": "generating|waiting|complete|error", ...}
}

# Stop/kill a session
# Args: session_id
driver_stop_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
}

# Interrupt session (Ctrl+C equivalent)
# Args: session_id
driver_interrupt_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
}
```

### Event Streaming
```bash
# Stream events from session
# Args: session_id, callback_function
# Callback receives: event_type, event_data
driver_stream_events() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local callback="$2"
    # Calls: $callback "question" "$question_json"
    # Calls: $callback "complete" "$status"
    # Calls: $callback "error" "$error_msg"
}
```

### Normalized Event Schema
Both drivers emit the same event format:
```json
{
  "type": "init|generating|waiting|complete|error",
  "session_id": "...",
  "timestamp": "2025-01-04T10:30:00Z",
  "wait_info": {
    "reason": "ask_user_question|agent_question_text|external_prompt|unknown",
    "context": "...",
    "options": ["a) ...", "b) ..."],
    "recommended": "a",
    "risk_level": "low|medium|high"
  }
}
```

## Driver Selection
```bash
detect_review_driver() {
    # Try ntm first (preferred)
    if command -v ntm &>/dev/null; then
        if ntm --robot-status &>/dev/null 2>&1; then
            log_verbose "Using ntm driver (robot mode available)"
            echo "ntm"
            return
        fi
        log_verbose "ntm found but robot mode unavailable"
    fi

    # Fall back to local driver
    if command -v tmux &>/dev/null && command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
        log_verbose "Using local driver (tmux + stream-json)"
        echo "local"
        return
    fi

    log_error "No suitable driver available"
    echo "unsupported"
}
```

## Driver Loading
```bash
load_review_driver() {
    local driver="$1"
    
    case "$driver" in
        ntm)
            source "$RU_LIB_DIR/drivers/ntm.sh"
            ;;
        local)
            source "$RU_LIB_DIR/drivers/local.sh"
            ;;
        *)
            log_error "Unknown driver: $driver"
            return 1
            ;;
    esac
    
    REVIEW_DRIVER="$driver"
}
```

## Implementation Notes
- Functions prefixed with driver_ are implemented per-driver
- Common code calls driver_* functions
- Drivers can extend interface with driver-specific features
- Capabilities check enables graceful feature degradation

## Testing
- Mock driver for unit tests
- Verify interface consistency between drivers
- Test driver selection logic
- Verify event normalization

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Interface defined with all required operations
- [ ] Driver selection works correctly
- [ ] Event schema documented and consistent
- [ ] Capabilities query implemented
- [ ] Driver loading mechanism works","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:35:14.284949263-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:35:14.284949263-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-jm89","depends_on_id":"bd-zlws","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:44:54.767331277-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-k1kx","title":"Implement ntm driver using robot mode API","description":"Task: Implement ntm Driver Using Robot Mode API

Purpose
-------
Implement the unified session driver interface using ntm robot mode,
providing advanced orchestration capabilities beyond the local driver.

ntm Robot Mode API
------------------
ntm exposes a JSON API via command-line flags:

1. --robot-spawn SESSION_NAME
   Create new tmux session with Claude panes
   Returns: {"success": true, "session": "...", "panes": [...]}

2. --robot-status
   Query all session states
   Returns: {"sessions": {...}, "questions": [...], "system": {...}}

3. --robot-health=SESSION
   Get health metrics for session
   Returns: {"agents": {...}, "alerts": [...]}

4. --robot-activity=PANE_ID
   Get activity state for specific pane
   Returns: {"state": "GENERATING", "velocity": 12.5, "confidence": 0.85}

5. --robot-route=SESSION
   Get recommended pane for new work
   Returns: {"recommendation": {"pane_id": "...", "reason": "..."}}

6. --robot-send --pane=PANE_ID --msg=MESSAGE
   Send message to pane
   Returns: {"status": "ok", "delivered": true}

Implementation
--------------

ntm_driver_start_session()
  - Call ntm --robot-spawn with --cc=$parallel for Claude panes
  - Record pane assignments in state
  - Return session info JSON

ntm_driver_get_session_state()
  - Call ntm --robot-activity for pane
  - Map ntm states to unified states
  - Include wait_info if WAITING

ntm_driver_send_to_session()
  - Call ntm --robot-send with pane and message
  - Verify delivery confirmation

ntm_driver_stream_events()
  - Poll --robot-activity periodically
  - Detect state transitions
  - Call callback on significant events

ntm_driver_stop_session()
  - Use tmux kill-session (ntm manages cleanup)

State Mapping
-------------
ntm state      -> Unified state
GENERATING     -> generating
WAITING        -> waiting (with wait_info)
THINKING       -> thinking
STALLED        -> stalled
ERROR          -> error

Health Monitoring Integration
-----------------------------
Start background health monitor:
  while session active:
    health = ntm --robot-health
    for alert in health.alerts:
      handle_alert(alert)
    sleep 10

Advantages Over Local Driver
----------------------------
- Velocity-based detection (more accurate)
- Health monitoring built-in
- Routing for optimal pane selection
- Better stall detection
- Structured error patterns

Testing
-------
- Mock ntm commands for unit tests
- Integration test with real ntm
- Verify state mapping correct
- Verify health alerts handled

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] All driver interface methods implemented
- [ ] State mapping matches unified schema
- [ ] Health monitoring integrated
- [ ] Routing recommendation used
- [ ] Graceful fallback if ntm unavailable","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:38:45.086444687-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:38:45.086444687-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k1kx","depends_on_id":"bd-jm89","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:11.685975198-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-koxf","title":"Define review plan artifact schema and validation","description":"# Task: Define Review Plan Artifact Schema and Validation

## Purpose
Define the machine-readable JSON schema that Claude must produce for each review session. This artifact is the contract between the Plan phase and Apply phase.

## Background: Why a Structured Artifact?
- Apply phase needs to know what to do
- Can't scrape agent transcripts reliably
- Enables resume after interruption
- Provides audit trail
- Enables metrics without parsing text

## File Location
```
{worktree}/.ru/review-plan.json
```

## Schema (Version 1)

```json
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "run_id": "20250104-103000-12345",
  "repo": "owner/repo",
  "worktree_path": "/home/user/.local/state/ru/worktrees/.../owner_repo",
  
  "items": [
    {
      "type": "issue",
      "number": 42,
      "title": "Authentication fails on Windows",
      "priority": "high",
      "decision": "fix",
      "notes": "Root cause: path separator in auth.py:234",
      "risk_level": "low",
      "files_changed": ["src/auth.py"],
      "lines_changed": 5
    },
    {
      "type": "pr",
      "number": 15,
      "title": "Add Redis caching",
      "priority": "normal",
      "decision": "skip",
      "notes": "Out of scope - adds external dependency",
      "risk_level": "n/a"
    }
  ],
  
  "questions": [
    {
      "id": "q1",
      "prompt": "Should I refactor all path handling or just fix this case?",
      "options": [
        {"label": "Quick fix", "description": "Fix only auth.py (5 lines)"},
        {"label": "Full refactor", "description": "Modernize all paths (45 lines)"},
        {"label": "Skip", "description": "Not a priority"}
      ],
      "recommended": "Quick fix",
      "answered": true,
      "answer": "Quick fix",
      "answered_at": "2025-01-04T10:35:00Z"
    }
  ],
  
  "git": {
    "branch": "ru/review/20250104-103000-12345/owner-repo",
    "base_ref": "main",
    "commits": [
      {
        "sha": "abc123def456",
        "subject": "Fix Windows path handling in auth.py",
        "files": ["src/auth.py"],
        "insertions": 3,
        "deletions": 2
      }
    ],
    "tests": {
      "ran": true,
      "ok": true,
      "command": "make test",
      "output_summary": "12 tests passed",
      "duration_seconds": 45
    }
  },
  
  "gh_actions": [
    {
      "op": "comment",
      "target": "issue#42",
      "body": "Fixed in commit abc123. The issue was path separators on Windows..."
    },
    {
      "op": "close",
      "target": "issue#42",
      "reason": "completed"
    },
    {
      "op": "label",
      "target": "issue#42",
      "labels": ["fixed-in-main"]
    },
    {
      "op": "comment",
      "target": "pr#15",
      "body": "Thank you for the suggestion. After review, this is out of scope..."
    }
  ],
```

## Validation Function
```bash
validate_review_plan() {
    local plan_file="$1"
    
    # Must exist
    [[ ! -f "$plan_file" ]] && { echo "Plan file not found"; return 1; }
    
    # Must be valid JSON
    if ! jq empty "$plan_file" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "Invalid JSON"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Required top-level fields
    if ! jq -e '.schema_version and .repo and .items' "$plan_file" >/dev/null; then
        echo "Missing required fields: schema_version, repo, or items"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Schema version check
    local version
    version=$(jq -r '.schema_version' "$plan_file")
    if [[ "$version" != "1" ]]; then
        echo "Unsupported schema version: $version"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Items must have required fields
    if ! jq -e '.items | all(.type and .number and .decision)' "$plan_file" >/dev/null; then
        echo "Items missing required fields: type, number, or decision"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Validate decision values
    local invalid_decisions
    invalid_decisions=$(jq -r '.items[] | select(.decision | IN("fix","skip","needs-info","closed") | not) | .decision' "$plan_file")
    if [[ -n "$invalid_decisions" ]]; then
        echo "Invalid decision values: $invalid_decisions"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Validate gh_actions if present
    if jq -e '.gh_actions' "$plan_file" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if ! jq -e '.gh_actions | all(.op and .target)' "$plan_file" >/dev/null; then
            echo "gh_actions missing required fields: op or target"
            return 1
        fi
    fi
    
    echo "Valid"
    return 0
}
```

Purpose
-------
Run tests and lint before allowing push, ensuring code quality and
preventing broken commits from reaching the repository.

Background: Defense in Depth
----------------------------
AI can make mistakes. Quality gates catch:
- Syntax errors
- Test failures
- Lint violations
- Security issues (secrets in code)
- Build failures

Gate Types
----------

1. Test Gate
   Run project test suite
   Command: auto-detect or configured
   Failure: blocks push

2. Lint Gate
   Run linters/formatters
   Command: configured per-repo
   Failure: blocks push

3. Secret Scan Gate
   Check for exposed secrets
   Tool: gitleaks or regex fallback
   Failure: blocks push with warning

4. Build Gate (optional)
   Verify project builds
   Command: configured
   Failure: blocks push

Implementation
--------------

run_quality_gates()
  local wt_path="$1"
  local plan_file="$2"
  
  # Load per-repo policy
  local repo_id=$(jq -r .repo "$plan_file")
  local policy="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/review-policies.d/${repo_id//\//_}.conf"
  [[ -f "$policy" ]] && source "$policy"
  
  # Run lint if configured
  if [[ -n "$REVIEW_LINT_CMD" ]]; then
    log_step "Running lint: $REVIEW_LINT_CMD"
    if \! (cd "$wt_path" && eval "$REVIEW_LINT_CMD"); then
      log_error "Lint failed"
      return 1
    fi
  fi
  
  # Run tests
  run_test_gate "$wt_path" || return 1
  
  # Secret scanning
  run_secret_scan "$wt_path" || return 2  # 2 = needs review
  
  log_success "Quality gates passed"
  return 0

run_test_gate()
  local wt_path="$1"
  local test_cmd="${REVIEW_TEST_CMD:-}"
  
  # Auto-detect if not configured
  if [[ -z "$test_cmd" ]]; then
    if [[ -f "$wt_path/Makefile" ]] && grep -q "^test:" "$wt_path/Makefile"; then
      test_cmd="make test"
    elif [[ -f "$wt_path/package.json" ]]; then
      test_cmd="npm test"
    elif [[ -f "$wt_path/Cargo.toml" ]]; then
      test_cmd="cargo test"
    elif [[ -f "$wt_path/setup.py" || -f "$wt_path/pyproject.toml" ]]; then
      test_cmd="pytest"
    fi
  fi
  
  if [[ -n "$test_cmd" ]]; then
    log_step "Running tests: $test_cmd"
    if \! (cd "$wt_path" && eval "$test_cmd"); then
      log_error "Tests failed"
      return 1
    fi
  fi
  
  return 0

run_secret_scan()
  local wt_path="$1"
  
  if command -v gitleaks &>/dev/null; then
    log_step "Scanning for secrets with gitleaks"
    if \! gitleaks detect --source "$wt_path" --no-git; then
      log_error "Secrets detected in changes"
      return 1
    fi
  else
    # Regex fallback
    if git -C "$wt_path" diff HEAD~1..HEAD | \
       grep -qiE "password\s*=|api.?key\s*=|secret\s*=|token\s*="; then
      log_warn "Potential secrets detected"
      return 2  # Needs human review
    fi
  fi
  
  return 0

 {"id":"bd-mnu9","title":"Add cmd_review() command skeleton with argument parsing","description":"# Task: Add cmd_review() Command Skeleton

## Purpose
Create the entry point for all review functionality - the cmd_review() function that will orchestrate the entire review workflow.

## Implementation Details

### Location in ru script
Add after cmd_prune() (around line 3634), following existing command pattern.

### Function Structure
```bash
cmd_review() {
    local mode="auto"
    local parallel=4
    local dry_run="false"
    local resume="false"
    local apply="false"
    local push="false"
    local priority_threshold="all"
    local max_repos=""
    local max_runtime=""
    local max_questions=""

    # Parse arguments
    parse_review_args "$@"

    # Check prerequisites
    check_review_prerequisites || exit 3

    # Generate unique run ID
    REVIEW_RUN_ID="$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)-$$"

    # Acquire global lock
    acquire_review_lock || { log_error "Another review is running"; exit 1; }

    # Auto-detect driver
    [[ "$mode" == "auto" ]] && mode=$(detect_review_driver)

    # Discovery phase
    log_step "Scanning repositories for open issues and PRs..."
    local -a work_items
    discover_work_items work_items "$priority_threshold" "$max_repos"

    if [[ ${#work_items[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
        log_success "No work items need review"
        release_review_lock
        return 0
    fi

    # Show summary
    show_discovery_summary "${work_items[@]}"

    if [[ "$dry_run" == "true" ]]; then
        log_info "Dry run - exiting without starting sessions"
        release_review_lock
        return 0
    fi

    # ... orchestration phases follow
}
```
